contract.goal: Close DESIGN.md § 13 by (a) refining the Sub-Issue side's `**Source**:` line from PLAN.md file-root to a per-node line anchor (`#L<n>`), and (b) wiring PLAN.md writeback into `tasks generate --apply` so successful Linear creates surface as inline `([linear](URL))` annotations on Phase/Batch/Task lines in PLAN.md. After this lands, `linear-loom tasks generate` is fully round-tripping and Phase 5 is complete.
acceptance_criteria: plan-parser.ts: FlatNode + ParsedPhase/Batch/Task each carry `line: number` (1-based absolute line in PLAN.md). Off-by-one in extractPhasesSection's startLine fixed alongside (existing absLine was 1 less than the true source line).

plan-writeback.ts (new lib): exports applyLinearUrlsToPlan(planText, urlsByComposedKey) returning { text, updated_lines }. For each Phase/Batch/Task line whose composed_key has an entry, appends `([linear](URL))` or replaces the URL inside an existing annotation. Lines outside the `## Phases` block are untouched. Idempotent under re-application with the same map.
tasks.ts composeBodyFn: per-node source_url = github.com/<org>/<repo>/tree/<branch>/projects/<slug>/PLAN.md#L<line> using node.line.

tasks.ts --apply path: builds Map<composed_key, linear_url> from applied[] (creates and updates carry URL; archives don't), runs applyLinearUrlsToPlan, writes to disk only when updated_lines > 0. New writeFileFn injection on TasksContext for testability. Output JSON gains plan_writeback: { updated_lines: N }. Dry-run path unchanged.

Tests: parser +2 (line assertion + flattenPlan propagation); plan-writeback +8 (empty no-op, fresh annotation, idempotent re-run, URL replacement, mixed, preserves outside-Phases content, unrelated map, empty-URL skip); tasks +5 (dry-run leaves PLAN.md untouched, --apply writes annotations, --apply skips write when no URL-carrying ops, issueCreate request body carries line-anchored source_url, plan-writeback-failed surfaces on writer throw).
npm test → 822 passing (was 807 after U3; +15 net).
rules_applied: DESIGN.md § 13 (bidirectional cross-references; Sub-Issue **Source**: points at the PLAN.md section that defined the Task).
DESIGN.md § 12.7 (idempotency — repeated --apply is a no-op on PLAN.md text after the first).
disqualifiers: Heading-anchor source_url (#phase-1-design-md) — line-anchor is sufficient and brittle-prose-proof.
Removing URL annotations on archive — archives delete the PLAN.md line upstream; the annotation goes with it.
HTML-comment or sibling-line annotation formats — inline ([linear](URL)) only.
Writing URLs back into Linear Documents — Document-side provenance already shipped in U2.
Configurability of writeback (no --no-writeback flag) — defer until a real reason emerges.

actions: Extended plan-parser.ts: added `line: number` to FlatNode + ParsedPhase + ParsedBatch + ParsedTask interfaces. Plumbed absLine through phase/batch/task construction and flattenPlan propagation. Fixed an off-by-one in extractPhasesSection (startLine = i + 2 not i + 1; first content line is allLines[i+1] whose 1-based source line is i+2).

Wrote plan-writeback.ts: pure function applyLinearUrlsToPlan(planText, urlsByComposedKey) → { text, updated_lines }. Re-parses the plan internally so writeback is self-contained, walks flat nodes, maps composed_key → line via the newly-exposed line field, then per matching line: regex-strip any existing `([linear](...))` annotation, trim trailing whitespace, re-append fresh annotation. Idempotency falls out naturally: same URL yields rewritten === original after the roundtrip, no counter tick.

Refined tasks.ts composeBodyFn: replaced single sourceUrl with a per-node sourceUrlFor(node) helper that composes `<planUrlBase>#L<node.line>`. Phase/Batch/Task each get a URL pointing at their own line.

Wired tasks.ts --apply path: after applyDiffOps, builds urlsByComposedKey from applied[] (filtering empty linear_url — milestones return empty since Linear's API doesn't expose Milestone URLs), runs applyLinearUrlsToPlan, writes via injected writeFileFn only when updated_lines > 0. New error code plan-writeback-failed surfaces I/O failures. JSON output gains plan_writeback: { updated_lines }.

Added writeFileFn?: (path: string, contents: string) => void to TasksContext. Default uses writeFileSync. Existing --apply tests updated to inject a no-op writeFileFn (the writeback would have tried to write to /tmp/projects/my-thing/PLAN.md and exited 1).

Wrote +15 tests: plan-parser.test.ts +2 (line assertions + flattenPlan propagation); plan-writeback.test.ts +8 (empty map, fresh annotation, idempotent re-run, URL replacement, mixed annotation, preserves outside-Phases content, unrelated-key map, empty-string url); tasks.test.ts +5 (dry-run no-write, --apply writes with correct content + path, --apply skips write on no-URL-ops, issueCreate body carries line-anchored URL, plan-writeback-failed on writer throw).
Verified: npm test → 822 passing across 56 files (baseline 807 + 15). Panel: contract-fit + naming + test-unit all approved on first run; zero findings.
corrections: Initial wiring tried to pre-fill urlsByComposedKey from linearState.by_composed_key for nodes that already exist Linear-side, but LinearStateNode doesn't carry a url field (only linear_id). Walked the trade-off and dropped the pre-fill: in the realistic flow, PLAN.md annotations get committed to git alongside the first --apply, so re-runs find the annotations already in place. The only failure mode is manual-delete-then-rerun, which is a niche refresh case that can ship in a follow-up if it ever becomes a real papercut.

First test run after wiring the writeback broke two pre-existing --apply tests because they didn't inject writeFileFn and the default writeFileSync was hitting non-existent test fixture paths. Surfaced as exit-code 1 (plan-writeback-failed). Updated both tests to inject writeFileFn: () => {} no-ops, matching the U3 pattern of dependency-injecting IO at the verb boundary.

changes_since_previous: Fourth and final unit of Phase 5, stacked on the now-merged U3 (#43). With this PR merged, Phase 5 (Plan-to-tasks generation) closes: linear-loom tasks generate is fully round-tripping — read PLAN.md, fetch Linear state, diff, apply mutations, write Linear URLs back into PLAN.md so the markdown surfaces the Linear-side state on re-runs. Phase 5 originally scoped as one branch; team executed in 4 units (parser → state fetch + diff → apply diff → bidirectional writeback) for reviewability.
notes_for_pr: Phase has no Linear URL — Linear's API doesn't expose a standalone URL for ProjectMilestones, only for Issues. So the writeback annotates Batch and Task lines but not Phase headings. PLAN.md still gets bidirectional reach for the issue-shaped nodes; the Phase-heading bullet from § 13 lands as 'effectively but not literally' (the Phase's milestone is reachable from any of its Batches' URLs). Surfaced here as a known asymmetry between § 13's prose and the Linear API's surface.

Pre-fill from existing Linear state was considered and dropped (see Corrections). The realistic flow commits annotations alongside first --apply, so re-runs find them in place. Manual-delete-then-rerun stays un-recoverable until a future refresh flag.

extractPhasesSection's startLine was off-by-one (absLine was 1 less than true source line). Only used in error messages today, so the bug went undetected — no test asserted specific line numbers. Fixed alongside the line-exposure work because the contract's '1-based absolute line' demands correctness. No test message-content broke since the existing parse-error tests only check the error code, not the line number text.

scope-shift signal noted: phase-boundary fires (this unit is the last in Phase 5). Single signal — no two-signal concurrence — so no inner-RPI offered. Recording the signal here per the loop's single-signal convention.

Substrate follow-up: bin/guild derive-panel returns 'panel-spec-unreadable (using fallback)' when invoked via the marketplace-installed CLI from a consumer plugin's source tree. Workaround used here: invoked via `node plugins/guild/cli/guild.ts` locally. Symptom is documented in the agents repo's session memory (feedback_loom_pr_opened_orphan_and_guild_spec_path); the substrate fix would be plugin-install-root resolution in the panel-spec lookup.

Substrate follow-up: this repo has no `npm run lint` or `npm run build` scripts. The vitest test run does the typecheck inline via vite, but the evaluator packet's pre-computed-verification section calls for separate lint/build lines per docs/SUBSTRATE-COMPOSITIONS.md § Compose dense packet. The fallback is to note 'not available' in the packet; longer-term, either ship the scripts or update the substrate's packet guidance to be repo-tolerant.

Substrate follow-up: the loom checkin-write substrate enforces append-once immutability (writeCheckin throws checkin-already-exists on overwrite), which conflicts with the ev-loop-interactive skill body's 'write contract-only at unit start, append execution at unit end' framing. This unit followed Pattern A (per the substrate's memory feedback_loom_phase_and_checkin_gaps): held contract in chat, wrote the complete checkin once at unit end. Cleaner than Pattern B (write twice with manual file edit), no event-trail noise.
